seo.page_title: Mercedes EQE 350 AMG Line 2024 import to NZ
seo.meta_description: Import a 2024 Mercedes EQE 350 AMG Line EV to NZ with 90.6 kWh battery, up to 590 km WLTP range and AMG Line trim, typically for less than local dealer pricing.
seo.h1_tag: Import a 2024 Mercedes EQE 350 AMG Line to NZ
seo.og_title: Mercedes EQE 350 AMG Line 2024 – Import to NZ
seo.og_description: Looking at a 2024 Mercedes EQE 350 AMG Line? We source from Japan with battery health reporting, clear auction docs and typical savings vs NZ dealer pricing.
seo.primary_keyword: Mercedes EQE 350 AMG Line import NZ

blocks.hero_h1: Import a 2024 Mercedes EQE 350 AMG Line to NZ
blocks.hero_subtitle: Bring in a 2024 Mercedes EQE 350 AMG Line from Japan with a 90.6 kWh battery, up to 590 km WLTP range and AMG Line trim, typically at a lower total cost than NZ retail.
proof_points: Transparent pricing — compliance is part of the process
blocks.why_kiwis_buy: Kiwis look at the 2024 Mercedes EQE 350 AMG Line for its 90.6 kWh battery, up to 590 km WLTP range and quiet motorway manners. It handles stop‑start city traffic smoothly, yet still does longer runs like Auckland–Hamilton or Wellington–Palmerston North with minimal planning around charging.

savings_block.headline: What you could save vs NZ dealer pricing
savings_block.body: Recent 2024 Mercedes EQE 350 AMG Line examples in Japan sit around $120,000–$145,000 NZD landed once you include shipping, taxes and NZ entry work. Local dealer pricing for similar cars is typically higher, especially for low‑km AMG Line models. We itemise auction price, shipping, insurance and compliance‑related costs so you can compare like‑for‑like with NZ retail.

cta_form.headline: Find your EQE 350 AMG Line — we'll do the searching
cta_form.subtext: Fill in your details to get started. We'll search the Japanese auctions and come back with real options and pricing by email.

blocks.video_caption: Take a look at the EQE 350 AMG Line’s cabin, with the MBUX screen layout and AMG Line steering wheel that you’ll be using every day.
blocks.main_usp_deep: The 2024 Mercedes EQE 350 AMG Line pairs a 90.6 kWh battery with a single motor rated at 288 hp and about 530 Nm, giving you 0–100 km/h in around 6.2 seconds without using any non-EV. WLTP range of up to 590 km means most NZ commutes and many weekend trips are covered on a single charge, especially on flatter routes like Christchurch or Hamilton. Adaptive cruise control, automatic emergency braking and a surround view camera help with day‑to‑day driving and tight parking. Inside, the MBUX system and premium audio make it easy to live with as a daily EV, not just a tech showpiece.

buyer_persona.headline: Is this car right for you?
buyer_persona.body: You’re likely a tech‑minded professional who wants a premium sedan that runs on electrons, not non-EV, and fits into a busy work and family week. You care about a clear battery health report, real‑world range for NZ trips and a cabin that works as a mobile office. If you like your EV to feel familiar yet modern, the EQE 350 AMG Line is a solid match.

trust_company.headline: Why import with ImportYourCar.co.nz?
trust_company.body: Since 2009, we’ve helped Kiwis bring in hundreds of vehicles direct from Japan, including many late‑model EVs. You see the same auction sheets we do, along with our notes on battery health, condition and likely landed cost. We handle the process from bidding and shipping through to NZ entry certification steps. You stay in control of the decisions, with a local team to talk things through.

car_specs_benefits.benefit: Gives you a large usable capacity for longer NZ trips and fewer charging stops on the motorway.
Delivers strong, smooth acceleration for safe overtakes and easy merging onto busy motorways.
Instant pull from low speed makes hill starts and short on‑ramps straightforward, even with passengers and luggage.
Covers most daily driving for a week for many NZ households, and makes inter‑city trips more relaxed.

car_specs_benefits.spec: Adaptive cruise, AEB, surround view camera
car_specs_benefits.benefit: Helps with long highway runs, heavy traffic and tight city parking, reducing day‑to‑day driving effort.

models_comparison.intro: Here’s how different 2024 Mercedes EQE 350 AMG Line options from Japan can vary for NZ buyers.

rows.notes: The EQE 350 AMG Line starts from 2024 in this shape, so you’re looking at very new cars that in most cases meet NZ entry standards if documentation is clean and no modifications are noted. Being new also helps with remaining manufacturer battery and vehicle warranty.

Most Japanese EQE 350 AMG Line cars use the 90.6 kWh battery with a single motor and rear‑wheel drive, though some markets offer 4MATIC all‑wheel drive. RWD suits NZ city and motorway use well, while 4MATIC can appeal if you often drive in wet or hilly conditions.

AMG Line brings sportier exterior styling, wheels and interior trim over standard EQE models. Some cars add options like upgraded audio, larger wheels or extra driver assistance features, which we’ll highlight in the auction notes so you can weigh value vs ride comfort and tyre costs.

As a late‑model EV, the EQE 350 AMG Line typically meets NZ entry standards if documentation is clean and no modifications are noted. There’s no exhaust emissions testing, but EV‑specific systems still need to pass inspection. We factor any EV‑related checks into the landed cost estimate.

The EQE 350 AMG Line is available in Japan but not in huge numbers, so you may wait a little for the right colour, mileage and option mix. Higher‑spec AMG Line cars with low km tend to attract more bidding competition, which can nudge auction prices up.

steps.title: Tell us what you're after
steps.body: Fill in the form or message us. We confirm your brief, talk through range and charging needs, and give you a cost breakdown before anything moves forward.

steps.title: We search the auctions
steps.body: We monitor USS, TAA, HAA and others for suitable EQE 350 AMG Line listings. We check battery‑related notes and any EV system comments on each shortlisted car, and flag anything that looks unusual.

steps.title: You approve, we bid
steps.body: We send you the auction sheet, photos and our recommendation. Once you're happy with the target car and budget, you pay a deposit and we bid on your behalf.

steps.title: Shipping to NZ
steps.body: Typically 2–4 weeks depending on the sailing schedule. For less common colour or option combinations, allow a little extra time once the auction clears. We keep you updated along the way.

steps.title: Compliance and delivery
steps.body: The car goes through NZ entry certification and compliance. Once it's through, we arrange delivery or you can collect, and we can talk through day‑one charging and setup.

risks_solutions.intro: Importing a late‑model EV is straightforward, but it helps to be clear on the main moving parts.

items.risk: You’re unsure how much the battery has degraded and what that means for future range.
items.solution: We focus on low‑km, newer EQE 350 AMG Line cars and review battery‑related notes on the auction sheet and any service records. You see this information before bidding so you can decide if the trade‑off between price, age and range works for you.

items.risk: Hidden costs between auction price and keys‑in‑hand in NZ.
items.solution: We break down auction price, shipping, insurance, NZ taxes, port charges and entry certification steps in a single landed estimate. That way you can compare against NZ dealer pricing using a realistic total, not just the hammer price.

items.risk: Uncertainty about condition and auction grades on a premium EV.
items.solution: You get the original Japanese auction sheet, photos and our plain‑English explanation of the grade, panel notes and interior comments. For an EQE 350 AMG Line we pay close attention to wheel damage, interior wear and any EV system remarks.

blocks.how_to_use_iyc: You tell us the sort of EQE 350 AMG Line you’re after — budget, colour preferences, mileage and options. We scan the Japanese auctions, filter out the risky cars and come back with a shortlist, landed cost estimates and our notes. You choose if and when to bid, and we handle the logistics from there.

faq_block.q: What real‑world range can I expect from a 2024 Mercedes EQE 350 AMG Line in NZ?
faq_block.a: The official figure is up to 590 km WLTP, but real‑world range in NZ will vary with speed, temperature and terrain. On flatter routes like Hamilton or Christchurch, many drivers will see range close to the WLTP number; in hilly or windy areas you should plan on less and allow a buffer.

faq_block.q: How much does it cost to import a 2024 EQE 350 AMG Line from Japan?
faq_block.a: Most examples land between about $120,000 and $145,000 NZD, depending on mileage, options and auction competition. That total includes the auction price, shipping, insurance, NZ taxes, port charges and entry certification‑related costs.

faq_block.q: Will a Japanese‑import EQE 350 AMG Line meet NZ entry standards?
faq_block.a: A 2024 EQE 350 AMG Line is very new and, based on auction documentation, this model usually meets NZ entry standards if documentation is clean and no modifications are noted. Final approval depends on physical inspection in NZ, so we never promise a typically goes through compliance before it’s checked.

faq_block.q: How long does it take from winning an auction to driving the car in NZ?
faq_block.a: Allow roughly 6–10 weeks in total. Shipping is typically 2–4 weeks depending on sailings, with the rest taken up by export handling in Japan, port processing in NZ and entry certification. Delays can happen around shipping schedules and inspection workloads.

faq_block.q: How do auction grades relate to the condition of a premium EV like this?
faq_block.a: Auction grades give a quick view of exterior and interior condition, but the comments and diagrams matter just as much. We translate the key points, highlight any repairs, wheel damage or interior wear, and share all documentation so you can judge whether the condition matches the price.

blocks.company_positioning: ImportYourCar.co.nz is an NZ‑based import service that’s been sourcing vehicles from Japan since 2009. We handle a mix of EVs, hybrids and non-EV vehicles, with a growing focus on late‑model electric cars like the EQE 350 AMG Line. Our role is to connect you with the Japanese auction market and manage the process through to NZ entry.

landed_cost_breakdown.intro: Your landed cost is the real, keys‑in‑hand figure — not just the auction hammer price.

rows.notes: What the EQE 350 AMG Line sells for on the day in Japan, plus local auction fees.
Ocean freight from Japan to NZ, marine insurance and handling at both ports.
NZTA entry certification, inspections, registration, on‑road costs and any EV‑specific checks required.

auction_checklist.check: Battery health notes and EV system warnings
auction_checklist.why: Gives early clues about how the 90.6 kWh battery has been treated and whether there are any logged issues.
Unusually high or low mileage for a 2024 car can change how you think about price, warranty and future range.
Kerb rash and scrapes are common on larger wheels; repairs can be costly on a premium EV sedan.

auction_checklist.check: Interior wear on seats, steering wheel and controls
auction_checklist.why: Heavy wear can signal hard use, even if the mileage looks low on paper.

auction_checklist.check: Presence of charging cables and accessories
auction_checklist.why: Missing cables add to your setup cost once the car arrives in NZ.

not_for_you_block.headline: Who this may not suit
items: You want a low‑cost runabout and don’t need a premium EV sedan.
You often tow heavy loads or need a large SUV‑style boot space.
You’re uncomfortable with the idea of importing and would rather pay extra for a car you can see on a local dealer forecourt.
You do frequent very long rural trips where public charging is limited and prefer a simpler charging setup.

alternatives_block.intro: If the EQE 350 AMG Line doesn’t quite line up with your needs, there are a few other imports worth a look.

rows.better_if: You want a slightly sportier drive feel and prefer BMW’s iDrive infotainment layout.
rows.tradeoff: You may see slightly less range and miss out on the AMG‑style design cues of the EQE.

rows.better_if: You prioritise maximum range and access to the Supercharger network for long‑distance travel.
rows.tradeoff: The interior is more minimalist and some buyers prefer the more traditional luxury feel of the Mercedes cabin.

rows.better_if: You want sharper handling and a more aggressive exterior design.
rows.tradeoff: Rear seat space is tighter and pricing is often higher than an EQE 350 AMG Line at similar mileage.

battery_health_block.headline: Battery health on the EQE 350 AMG Line
battery_health_block.body: With a 90.6 kWh pack, the EQE 350 AMG Line has plenty of capacity, but long‑term health still matters. We focus on newer, low‑km cars and use auction documentation and service notes to spot signs of heavy fast‑charging or EV system warnings. You get this information up front so you can balance price against expected range and long‑term ownership.
checklist: Confirm build date and first registration to understand remaining battery warranty.
Check for any EV or high‑voltage system warnings on the auction sheet.
Look at mileage and usage pattern to gauge likely battery wear.
Prefer cars with regular servicing at recognised dealers where possible.
Avoid cars with repeated notes about charging issues or reduced range.

charging_block.headline: Charging a Mercedes EQE 350 AMG Line in NZ
charging_block.home_charging: At home, most owners will use an AC wall unit on a Type 2 connection. Overnight charging is usually enough to top up daily use, and for many households a weekly deep charge will cover commuting and local running around. We can talk through cable options and what to ask your electrician.
charging_block.public_charging: On the road, the EQE 350 AMG Line uses CCS Combo for DC fast charging, which is supported across most major NZ charging networks. For inter‑city trips, a quick top‑up at a fast charger can add useful range while you grab a coffee, especially if you start with a near‑full battery.
caveats: Fast‑charging too often can add wear to any EV battery over time, so it’s best kept for trips rather than daily use.
Charging speeds can vary by charger brand, temperature and how full the battery already is.
If you live in an apartment or shared parking, you’ll need to confirm what charging options are allowed before committing.

blocks.range_real_world: In NZ conditions, the EQE 350 AMG Line’s up to 590 km WLTP figure will shift with how and where you drive. Flat city and motorway running in places like Hamilton or Christchurch will usually be closest to the lab number, especially at steady speeds. In hillier or windier areas like Wellington, or if you sit at higher motorway speeds, you should plan for a lower usable range and keep a buffer for weather and detours.
blocks.ev_running_costs: Day‑to‑day running costs on an EQE 350 AMG Line are typically well below a similar non-EV sedan. Most home charging setups mean a full charge costs a fraction of filling a tank, and there’s no motor oil or exhaust system to service. You’ll still have tyres, brakes, cabin filters and software updates to think about, but overall servicing is usually simpler and more predictable over time.
blocks.model_deep_dive: The 2024 Mercedes EQE 350 AMG Line sits in the premium EV sedan space, with a 90.6 kWh battery, single‑motor rear‑wheel drive and 288 hp output. It’s tuned for quiet, comfortable cruising rather than outright performance, but 0–100 km/h in around 6.2 seconds is still brisk. AMG Line trim adds sportier styling and interior touches, while the MBUX system brings a modern interface and connected features. For NZ buyers, it’s a way to get into a late‑model Mercedes EV with long range and a familiar luxury feel.
blocks.trim_comparison: Within the EQE range, AMG Line models from Japan usually sit above standard trims in terms of styling and equipment. You’ll often see larger wheels, different bumpers and upgraded interior materials. Some cars add further options like enhanced audio or extra driver assistance features; others keep things simpler, which can help ride comfort and reduce wheel and tyre replacement costs. We’ll point out these differences in each auction listing so you can decide what matters most.
blocks.not_for_you: If you’re after a basic commuter EV or a compact hatch, the EQE 350 AMG Line may feel like more car and more budget than you need. It’s also not ideal if you regularly drive on rough gravel roads where a higher‑riding SUV would be more comfortable. And if you’re set on a physical test drive before deciding, importing may not suit how you like to buy cars.
